skill.name: relight
skill.description: Relight a still image — change the lighting setup, color temperature, direction, or mood — on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI. Routes to Qwen Edit 2509's dedicated relight LoRA endpoint for purpose-built relighting, with fallback to identity-preserving edit endpoints when prose lighting language is enough. Use for product relighting (studio softbox to window light), portrait mood shift (overcast to golden hour), or color-grade change.
skill.summary: Change image lighting using AI - adjust direction, color temperature, and mood through natural language prompts on the RunComfy platform.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All 93 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation and instructional guide for using the RunComfy runcomfy CLI to relight images with AI models. The backtick external_commands flags are markdown code fence blocks showing example CLI usage, not Ruby shell execution. All network URL flags point to legitimate RunComfy platform pages, documentation, or cross-skill references. Filesystem flags reference documentation about token storage location. Weak cryptographic algorithm flags are on plain markdown headers and documentation text. No malicious patterns, prompt injection attempts, or data exfiltration mechanisms were detected. The skill is safe to publish.

content.value_statement: Changing image lighting traditionally requires a full reshoot or complex editing software. This skill lets you transform lighting direction, color temperature, and mood using natural language prompts through the RunComfy platform.
actual_capabilities: Change lighting direction including key light, fill light, and rim light placement
Adjust color temperature between warm and cool tones
Apply time-of-day lighting effects such as golden hour, blue hour, and overcast
Preserve subject identity, pose, and framing during relighting
Route to specialized AI models for optimal relighting results
Match lighting style from a reference photo using multi-image editing
limitations: Cannot generate new images from scratch
Requires a RunComfy account and API token
Limited to image relighting only; cannot add or remove objects

use_cases.title: Product Catalog Relighting
use_cases.description: Apply uniform lighting across an entire product catalog. Transform images from white-box studio to lifestyle window light for consistent brand presentation.

use_cases.title: Portrait Mood Transformation
use_cases.description: Shift the mood and atmosphere of portrait photographs. Change overcast lighting to golden hour or add dramatic studio lighting effects.

use_cases.title: Batch Lighting Consistency
use_cases.description: Apply the same lighting prompt across multiple product images in a single batch. Maintain consistent lighting for an entire SKU gallery.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Relight
prompt_templates.prompt: Relight this image to golden hour lighting with warm tones.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user wanting a quick lighting change

prompt_templates.title: Studio Product Relight
prompt_templates.prompt: Relight this product image with warm 3200K key light from camera-left at 45 degrees, soft cool fill from right, no rim light. Preserve product orientation and color identity.

prompt_templates.title: Portrait Mood Shift
prompt_templates.prompt: Relight this portrait with golden hour rim from behind, warm soft key from front-left. Preserve subject identity.

prompt_templates.title: Reference-Based Lighting Match
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 Edit with a reference image. Apply the lighting direction, color temperature, and contrast of the reference photo to this image. Preserve the subject identity.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user matching lighting across photos

output_examples.input: Product photo taken with harsh overhead fluorescent lighting
output_examples.output: Same product with warm golden-hour window light from the left, soft shadows on the counter, preserved product orientation and color identity

output_examples.input: Portrait taken on an overcast day with flat, cool lighting
output_examples.output: Same person with golden hour rim lighting from behind, warm soft key light from the front, preserved facial features and expression

best_practices: Include preservation language in your prompt such as preserve subject pose and color identity to prevent unwanted changes
Start with time-of-day shortcuts like golden hour or blue hour for consistent and predictable results
Use JSON input format for complex multi-light setups specifying direction, temperature, and intensity for each light source
anti_patterns: Using the relight endpoint for edits that are not about lighting, such as adding objects or changing backgrounds
Omitting preservation language in prompts, which can cause the model to drift from the original subject appearance
Passing untrusted image URLs from third-party sources without verifying the content first

faq.question: What kind of lighting changes can this skill make?
faq.answer: You can change lighting direction, color temperature, intensity, and mood. This includes effects like golden hour, studio softbox, blue hour, and multi-light setups with key fill and rim lights.

faq.question: Do I need a RunComfy account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a RunComfy account and API token. You can sign in using the runcomfy login command or set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: Can I relight multiple images at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can batch relight multiple images by passing an image_urls array with the same lighting prompt applied across the batch.

faq.question: What is the difference between Qwen Edit Relight and the other models?
faq.answer: Qwen Edit Relight is a purpose-built LoRA for dedicated relighting. The other models are better for broader edits that include lighting changes as part of a larger modification.

faq.question: Will relighting change my subject appearance?
faq.answer: The models are designed to preserve subject identity. You should include preservation language in your prompt like preserve subject pose to maintain consistent appearance.

faq.question: How do I match the lighting of a reference photo?
faq.answer: Use GPT Image 2 Edit with both images as input. Include both the source image and the lighting reference image and describe the lighting transfer.
